Section A
Negative marking: correct answers receive +1 and wrong answers receive -1/3. Unanswered questions receive 0.

Question 1. Which statement best explains the difference between signal potency and signal efficacy?
A. Potency is the maximal effect and efficacy is the concentration required.
B. Potency concerns the concentration needed for a response, while efficacy concerns maximal response.
C. Potency and efficacy are always identical.
D. Efficacy is only measured in tables. [1]

Question 2. Calculate the fold change from the graph if response rises from 2 to 8 arbitrary units. [2]

Section B
Question 3. Define a regulatory feedback loop and list two source-linked examples. [4]

Question 4. A new protein-like system changes shape after mutation. Design an experiment to test folding integrity, state the expected readout, and include one control. [12]

Question 5. Discuss how a source-linked mechanism can be converted into an essay argument using evidence and limitations. [20]
